steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the radius from the circumference
steps.content:
Given circumference $C = 18\pi$, use the formula $C = 2\pi r$:
$$18\pi = 2\pi r$$
$$r = 9 \text{ meters}$$

steps.title: Calculate the volume
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 9$ and $h = 7$ into the cylinder volume formula:
$$V = \pi(9)^2(7) = \pi \cdot 81 \cdot 7 = 567\pi \text{ cubic meters}$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
